zh.lifeSummary: Isabelle Jeanne de Rodes是Gerad Lii de Rodes和Clarisse van Eksaarde的女兒，於1328年出生在比利時維斯吉福拉安德倫英爾姆斯特。她與Jean “le Grand Doigt” de Ghistelles結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1362年在比利時維斯吉福拉安德倫希斯特爾享年34歲，埋葬在法國米迪-列內斯格斯拉德艾-維爾艾格利 沙吉皮。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Isabelle Jeanne de Rodes是Gerad Lii de Rodes和Clarisse van Eksaarde的女儿，于1328年出生在比利时維斯吉福拉安德倫英爾姆斯特。她与Jean “le Grand Doigt” de Ghistelles结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1362年在比利时維斯吉福拉安德倫希斯特爾享年34岁，埋葬在法国米迪-列內斯格斯拉德艾-維爾艾格利 沙吉皮。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Rodes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Catalan (Rodés): habitational name from any of several places called Rodés, mainly those in El Pallars and El Conflent districts, in northern Catalonia.
Catalan: variant of Roda , from Catalan rodes, plural of roda ‘wheel’.
English: variant of   Rhodes  . Compare   Rhode  .

variants.name: Rudes
meanings: Americanized form of Croatian and Slovenian Rudež: nickname derived from rud ‘red, reddish’, or, alternatively, from rud ‘curly’ (standard Slovenian rod; compare Rodes ). In Croatia, the surname is also found in the form Rudeš.

variants.name: Roda
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Arturo, Francisco, Domingos, Manuel, Mario, Alfredo, Amandio, Andres, Angel, Eduardo, Elia, Gustavo, Joaquim.

Catalan: habitational name from any of various places called Roda, for example Roda de Ter, Roda de Berà, Roda de Ribagorça, from Catalan roda ‘wheel’ (from Latin rota), possibly denoting a waterwheel.
German: habitational name from any of numerous places, particularly in central eastern Germany, named Roda or Rhoda.

variants.name: Rodas
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Luis, Jorge, Juan, Ana, Jaime, Rafael, Elvira, Manuel, Miguel, Raul.
Galician and Portuguese: habitational name from any of the three places in Galicia, Spain, named Rodas (in Lugo, Ourense, and Pontevedra provinces), from the plural of roda ‘wheel’.

nameMeanings.name: Isabelle
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isabel
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

nameMeanings.name: Jeanne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Jane
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: Isabelle Jeanne de Rodes was born in 1328, in Ingelmunster, West Flanders, Belgium as the daughter of Gerad Lii de Rodes and Clarisse van Eksaarde. She married Jean “le Grand Doigt” de Ghistelles. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1362, in Gistel, West Flanders, Belgium, at the age of 34, and was buried in Église Saint-Pierre, Ladevèze-Ville, Gers, Midi-Pyrénées, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Isabelle Jeanne de Rodes est née en 1328, à Ingelmunster, Flandre occidentale, Belgique, fille de Gerad Lii de Rodes et Clarisse van Eksaarde. Elle a épousé Jean “le Grand Doigt” de Ghistelles. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée en 1362, à Gistel, Flandre occidentale, Belgique, à l’âge de 34 ans, et a été enterrée à Église Saint-Pierre, Ladevèze-Ville, Gers, Midi-Pyrénées, France.

ko.lifeSummary: Isabelle Jeanne de Rodes(은)는 1328년 벨기에 웨스트-블라아더 잉먼스터에서 Gerad Lii de Rodes(와)과 Clarisse van Eksaarde의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 Jean “le Grand Doigt” de Ghistelles(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1362년 벨기에 웨스트-블라아더 지스텔에서 34세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 미디-p이ee스 그르스 라데브e즈-빌 e그리스 세인트-피르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Isabelle Jeanne de Rodes nasceu em 1328, em Ingelmunster, Flandres Ocidental, Bélgica filha de Gerad Lii de Rodes e Clarisse van Eksaarde. Ela casou-se com Jean “le Grand Doigt” de Ghistelles. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 1362, em Gistel, Flandres Ocidental, Bélgica, com 34 anos, e foi sepultada em Église Saint-Pierre, Ladevèze-Ville, Gers, Midi-Pyrénées, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Isabelle Jeanne de Rodes родилась в 1328 г. в местности ингелмунстир, вест-влаандирен, Бельгия. Её родителей звали Gerad Lii de Rodes и Clarisse van Eksaarde. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Jean “le Grand Doigt” de Ghistelles. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в 1362 г. в местности гистел, вест-влаандирен, Бельгия в возрасте 34 лет и была похоронена в местности eглис сеинт-пьирр, ладевeзе-вилл, джирс, миди-пирeнeесь, Франция.
